task_completion.description: The Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive application is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The application window for 'Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive' is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Olaparib is selected as the cancer drug.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 'Olaparib' is visible in the selected cancer drugs list, search bar, or input field.

task_completion.description: Carbamazepine, Warfarin, and Acenocoumarol are selected as co-medications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 'Carbamazepine', 'Warfarin', and 'Acenocoumarol' are all visible in the selected co-medications list or input fields.

task_completion.description: The interaction ratings for the selected medications are displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A results screen or table is visible showing Olaparib cross-referenced with Carbamazepine, Warfarin, and Acenocoumarol, displaying their respective interaction ratings (e.g., color codes like Red for Carbamazepine).

task_completion.description: The Interaction Details screen for Olaparib and Carbamazepine is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A detailed view or popup is visible specifically for the 'Olaparib - Carbamazepine' interaction, showing clinical details such as CYP3A4 induction, AUC reduction, or contraindication warnings.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the graphical user interface of the specified application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show standard GUI elements (buttons, dropdowns, lists) being clicked or selected, rather than terminal commands or scripts executing the search.

integrity.description: The agent used the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app rather than a web browser or external database.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface belongs to the local Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app, not a web browser (like Chrome/Firefox) searching PubMed, DrugBank, or WebMD.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the expected answers into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interaction details and ratings are displayed within the app's native UI formatting, not as plain text in Notepad, TextEdit, or similar text editors.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Olaparib is a PARP inhibitor metabolized by CYP3A4. Carbamazepine is a strong CYP3A4 inducer. The interaction between Olaparib and Carbamazepine is contraindicated (typically a 'Red' rating) because carbamazepine reduces olaparib AUC by approximately 87%, eliminating its therapeutic efficacy. Warfarin and Acenocoumarol have minimal pharmacokinetic interactions with Olaparib as they are not primarily CYP3A4 substrates.
